examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Cliff,

Thank you so much for reaching out,

I'm Ruan from the Sales and Support team at Waveform, and I'd be more than happy to assist you in any way I can.

I want to make sure that we recommend the best solution for your needs. Before we proceed with a recommendation, there are a few general questions that will help us move on to the next steps that I’ve listed below:

What is the address where the setup will be done? (For reviewing cell tower maps.)

What are your current data speeds, with and without the antenna?

How would you describe your internet usage? (e.g., streaming, working from home, gaming, etc.)

Regarding upload speeds, it is very likely that you are connected to the n41 band. This frequency band uses time-sharing, which means that the tower spends most of its time sending data to you and less time receiving data from you, resulting in great download speeds but poor upload speeds. In that case, an antenna would only be able to boost your upload speeds to a certain threshold. 

As for the antenna location, I would always recommend installing a QuadPro outside, as having it aimed at a wall would impact the performance to an extent.

If you have any questions or concerns, please let me know!

Hope you have a lovely day.

Best,

examples.subject: Re: Follow Up
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Lee,

Thank you so much for reaching out,

I'm Ruan from the Sales and Support team at Waveform, and I'd be more than happy to assist you in any way I can.

I want to make sure that we recommend the best solution for your needs. Before we proceed with a recommendation, there are a few general questions that will help us move on to the next steps that I’ve listed below:

Which carrier are you using?

What is the address where the setup will be done? (For reviewing cell tower maps.)

What are your current data speeds, with and without the antenna?

How would you describe your internet usage? (e.g., streaming, working from home, gaming, etc.)

There should be a couple of different mounting options; please have a look at the detailed guide for the GL-X2000 with step-by-step instructions and pictures for your convenience here.

Regarding discount codes, there are no formal discount codes for these purchases just yet. You are welcome to use my personal discount code for 5% off your purchase (5OFFRDP). Additionally, we have the upcoming Spring Prime sale that starts 25 Mar!

If you have any questions or concerns, please let me know!

Hope you have a lovely day.

Best,

examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Cliff,

Thank you for providing me with this information.

Please find the map of the T-Mobile towers in your area; your location will be circled.

You can view this map here.

Looks like you have a couple of high-frequency towers close to your location; it seems like you are connected to the tower with the blue dot. 

You could aim to the towers to your north to avoid the n41 towers to your west. Please keep these directions in mind when installing an antenna system.

In your case, I would recommend the QuadPro, since the antenna is compatible with your gateway and is directional, meaning you could use it to pull signal from a different tower, which should help get those upload speeds up!

Since your video calls stutter when cloud backups run, it might help to schedule those backups for off-hours or adjust their upload bandwidth limit. That way, they’re less likely to compete with real-time calls and cause interruptions.

Let me know your thoughts on the recommendation.

Best,

sopMatches.excerpt:
Drift chat

Live chat with customers that are active on our website is a great opportunity to proactively engage customers while they're actively interested in our products, or in need of support. 

General Best Practices

There's an art to engaging customers with a live chat, and by the time you're live at Waveform you'll have had plenty of guidance and practice, so you should be a pro. 

Nonetheless, a couple notes: 

Documentation, documentation, documentation! Leave thorough notes in PipeDrive and Locate as necessary, to make life easier for yourself and your team down the road.
Chat is in

examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi John,

Thank you for your response.

The kit comes with two cables. Choosing one ten-foot cable means you will have one for each antenna, so no extra cables are needed.

The QuadPro is directional, not omni. With your setup, pairing a directional antenna with your current one is what's recommended, not using an omnidirectional.

If you decide on a full eight by eight, you will not be able to reduce the number of cables included. The kit always comes with two, so choosing one ten-foot cable still gives you two, one for each antenna. There's also no option to choose not to receive the second cable.

You could use your existing cable, but that would need extra adapters, and since the kit already includes a cable, it is simpler to use the one provided.

If its still unclear, can you please schedule a call with me here?

zcal.co/abeds

Best,

Info Gathering for Design

Once the project is qualified, we've built a strong rapport with the customer and we've got signal readings, we're almost ready to begin with the design. The next step is intensive info gathering, to ensure our design team has everything they need to draw up a good design for you. 

Here's how we go about info gathering 

1. Schedule a call with the customer to discuss the finer details that will be relevant to our design. 
Zoom works well, and 30 minutes is usually a good time estimate.

examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi John,

Thank you for reaching out to us. I'd be happy to help.

You can use your existing antenna and simply get a second one to pair with it, aiming both at the same tower. You won’t need a full 8x8 setup, since an 8x8 is essentially two antennas, and you already have one. You can check the price here.

Unfortunately, I wasn’t able to find an order under your details to see which antenna you have. Could you please let me know which antenna you are currently using?

Another option is to try our outdoor enclosure (price shown in the link). If you get the G5AR and test it outside, you might see improved speeds. You could then use the gateway inside the enclosure with a separate Wi-Fi router to cover your indoor space.

Best,

examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
John,

Thank you for your response. To answer your question, 1x 20 ft cable would be 2x for the 8x8. You can't connect them with just one 4x4 cable. They don't take into account anyone having a previous setup.

If you'd like to buy another 4x4 to create the 8x8 instead of a full 8x8 (to save you the money), I'd suggest going with this antenna:

[url]

It's the improved version of the antenna you have. It has similar gains but more flexible cables. It also comes with a window entry cable to avoid drilling holes.

Just place the new antennas close to the old antennas you have and aim them at the same tower.

Best,

sopMatches.title: Customer Service Etiquette
sopMatches.excerpt:
Customer Service Etiquette

Did you know?

73% of consumers say friendly customer service reps can make them fall in love with a brand. Right Now
42% of consumers switch brands because they are put off by rude or unhelpful staff New Voice

The Do's and Don'ts

Do's: 

Always answer the phone with your name and where you are calling from:
i.e "Hello! This is Ian from Waveform, thanks for reaching out! May I get your name?"
Always keep a friendly, mellow, and professional tone.
Begin and end every encounter on a positive note.
Use the hold button if research will take a while but be sure to chec
